faqs.q: Will converting PNG to JPG reduce file size?
faqs.a: Yes, significantly. JPG uses lossy compression, which means it permanently removes some data to make the file smaller. In most cases, converting a PNG to JPG can reduce the file size by 50% to 80% without noticeable quality loss.

faqs.q: Does converting to JPG remove background transparency?
faqs.a: Yes. The JPG format does not support transparency (alpha channels). When you convert a transparent PNG to JPG, the transparent areas will automatically be filled with a solid color, usually white.

faqs.q: Is it safe to convert my personal images here?

faqs.q: Which format is better for my website?
faqs.a: If the image is a photograph with many colors, use JPG to save bandwidth. If the image is a logo, graphic, or requires a transparent background, stick with PNG (or consider WebP for modern web support).

faqs.q: Does this work on mobile devices?

faqs.a: No. Converting a lossy format (JPG) to a lossless format (PNG) will not restore lost data or fix compression artifacts. It will only prevent further quality loss during future edits.

faqs.q: Will the file size increase when converting to PNG?
faqs.a: Yes. Because PNG is a lossless format, it stores pixel data much less efficiently than JPG. A JPG converted to PNG will almost always result in a significantly larger file.

faqs.q: Why should I convert my JPG to PNG?
faqs.a: If you plan to edit a photo multiple times, converting it to PNG prevents 'generation loss' (the gradual degradation of quality that occurs every time a JPG is saved).
